findings.description: The inclusive-language rule (use 'someone/people with dermatomyositis' not 'patients' outside HCP materials; use 'care team' not 'staff') has no corresponding rule in the candidate set. This is a distinct, testable brand rule that is entirely missing from the cluster.

payload.description: Do not use 'patients' in patient-facing materials (use someone/people with dermatomyositis); use 'care team' not 'staff'. 'Patients' allowed only in HCP materials.

findings.description: The numbers-formatting rule (spell out zero-nine, numerals 10+, exceptions for percentages/ages/ratios, % sign usage) is not represented by any candidate rule.

payload.description: Spell out zero-nine; use numerals for 10+. Always use numerals for percentages, ages, and ratios; use % sign for percentages.

findings.description: The Oxford/serial comma punctuation rule has no corresponding candidate rule; it is a discrete, unambiguous editorial constraint that was dropped from clustering.

payload.description: Always use the Oxford/serial comma in series of three or more items.

findings.description: The [GENERAL] scoped sub-rule stating every standalone section must re-establish 'dermatomyositis (DM)' at first mention, UNLESS the assembly brief states the abbreviation is established upstream, is not reflected in the rule's governance facets. The conditional override (assembly-brief exception) and the [GENERAL] scope tag are missing, making the rule appear unconditionally hard when it has a documented escape clause.

payload.conditional_exception: unless the assembly brief states the abbreviation is established upstream

findings.description: Source text uses imperative avoidance language ('avoid negative or fear-based language') which reads closer to a hard constraint on prohibited tone rather than a soft 'should' strong_default; polarity/hardness may understate the compliance requirement, particularly for the fear-based language prohibition portion versus the empathetic-tone guidance portion which are conflated into one rule.
